ksa_text: Define, document, and implement enterprise metric definitions in the semantic layer, ensuring consistency across BI tools, business intelligence platforms, and analytical consumers through metric governance.

dcwf.top_element_label: Utilize data structures to organize, sort, and manipulate elements of information

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Utilize data structures to organize, sort, and manipulate elements of information

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA DA-T-005 (DA, Task) does not clear the moderate fit inclusion threshold of 0.43. Composite score 0.4233 is insufficient to establish relevance to senior-level analytics and business intelligence at IC-to-senior-IC level. Framework signals (ddat=0.423, dcwf=0.409, onet=0.408) do not demonstrate sufficient alignment with core analytics/BI work.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.challenge: DA domain KSA DA-T-005 excluded at composite 0.4233, but KSA text contains direct analytics-domain signals. Internal consistency: the presence of analytics terminology in a moderate fit KSA at score 0.4233 suggests the threshold is excluding genuinely relevant content.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.rebuttal: Composite 0.4233 with confirmed analytics-domain terminology for a DA (moderate fit) KSA indicates real role relevance for Senior Data Analyst. The 0.43 threshold is a calibration heuristic; text-signal confirmation overrides marginal score shortfall at this level.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: P2 exclude overturned. DA KSA with composite 0.4233 and confirmed analytics-domain text signals included on internal consistency grounds.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External validation: does KSA DA-T-005 (DA, composite 0.4233) appear in published data analyst / BI role profiles and workforce frameworks?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: KSA content aligns with DCWF 421 and DDAT Data Architect profiles; DA competencies at awareness level appear in senior IC data analyst role profiles. Analytics-domain terminology in KSA text confirms practical relevance to IC ANL work. Composite 0.4233 with signal across ddat=0.423, dcwf=0.409, onet=0.408 is consistent with documented ANL competency expectations at IC-to-senior-IC level. External ground truth supports inclusion.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: P3 include upheld. External evidence from DA-domain frameworks confirms KSA relevance to Senior Data Analyst at IC-to-senior-IC level.

decision_rationale: Single adversarial overturn by P3 internal consistency. Final decision: include. Composite 0.4233, domain DA. Medium confidence — one agent challenged and overturned the preliminary assessment.
